  Prior work on model collapse primarily focuses on a single model trained on its own output, failing to capture model performance in multi-model interactive settings. In this work, we fill this gap by investigating the performance of generative models in an interactive learning environment with general interaction patterns. In particular, we formalize model interactions using directed graphs and show that the occurrence of model collapse depends critically on the topology of the interaction graph. We further derive an explicit necessary and sufficient condition characterizing when model collapse occurs, and establish finite-sample results for linear regression and asymptotic guarantees for general M-estimators. We support our theoretical findings through extensive numerical experiments.

</details>


### [508] [Atoms of Thought: Universal EEG Representation Learning with Microstates](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.20182)
*Xinyang Tian,Ruitao Liu,Ziyi Ye,Siyang Xue,Xin Wang,Xuesong Chen*

Main category: cs.LG

TL;DR: 本文提出基于微状态（microstates）的通用EEG表征方法，通过在大规模医疗EEG数据集上聚类得到微状态分词器，将连续EEG信号转化为离散微状态序列，并在睡眠分期、情绪识别、运动想象分类等多个下游任务中验证了其优于传统时域和频域特征，同时具有更好的可解释性和可扩展性。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 传统EEG表征学习依赖时域或频域特征，缺乏通用性和可解释性，作者希望探索一种更简洁、有效且通用的EEG表征方式。

Method: 通过聚类将大规模医疗EEG数据中的连续信号转化为离散微状态序列，构建通用微状态分词器，并将其应用于多个下游任务进行表征学习。

Result: 基于微状态的EEG表征在睡眠分期、情绪识别、运动想象分类等任务中，在不同模型下均优于传统时域和频域特征。

Conclusion: 微状态表征不仅性能优越，而且更具可解释性和可扩展性，有望在认知神经科学和临床研究中得到广泛应用。

Abstract: Learning universal representations from electroencephalogram (EEG) signals is a cutting-edge approach in the field of neuroinformatics and brain-computer interfaces (BCIs). Conventionally, EEG is treated as a multivariate temporal signal, where time- or frequency-domain features are extracted for representation learning. This paper investigates a simple yet effective EEG representation, i.e., microstates. Microstates represent the building blocks of brain activity patterns at a microscopic time scale. We build a universal microstate tokenizer from a large medical EEG dataset by clustering continuous EEG signals into sequences of discrete microstates. The microstate tokenizer is then adopted universally across a series of downstream tasks, including sleep staging, emotion recognition, and motor imagery classification. Experimental results show that EEG representation learning with microstates outperforms traditional time-domain and frequency-domain features under different models and across different tasks. Further analysis shows that microstates offer greater interpretability and scalability, thereby opening up applications in both cognitive neuroscience and clinical research.

</details>


<div id='cs.GT'></div>

# cs.GT [[Back]](#toc)

### [509] [The fitness landscape of social norms in social dilemmas](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.18834)
*Maximilian Puelma Touzel*

Main category: cs.GT

TL;DR: 本文在社会规范研究中，将Morsky & Akçay（2019）基于矩阵博弈的规范分类与理性准则理论推广到更一般的马尔可夫博弈（强化学习常用框架）中，澄清了基本要素，并给出了复制者动力学的通解与分析。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 社会规范通过多智能体间的行为协调解决社会困境，但现有理论多局限于矩阵博弈，缺乏对随机信号环境的更一般化处理。本文旨在将规范理论拓展到马尔可夫博弈框架下，以更好地适配强化学习等现代方法。

Method: 将Morsky & Akçay的规范分类与理性准则从矩阵博弈推广到马尔可夫博弈；通过信号-奖励空间映射规范；采用复制者动力学分析规范的演化与涌现。

Result: 给出了复制者动力学的通解；展示了规范在信号与奖励空间上的映射关系；阐明了相关均衡（而非纳什均衡）作为规范一致性条件的合理性。

Conclusion: 本文提供了一个更一般化的规范演化理论框架，将社会规范分析与强化学习中的马尔可夫博弈相结合，为多智能体系统中的规范涌现与协调提供了理论支撑。

Abstract: By specifying behaviour across multiple agents, social norms are a coordination approach to resolving social dilemmas. Decentralized and wide adoption can be achieved by norms whose prescription involves interpreting stochastic signals in the environment. Such signals must have enough correlation to orchestrate mutually beneficial coordination and enough disincentivizing uncertainty about the benefits of exploiting that coordination. Evolutionary game theory of matrix games has been used to describe how, by rational agents comparing and adopting norms, a norm can evolve to become dominant in a population. Morsky \& Akçay (2019) classify norms according to a set of rationality criteria. Joint player strategies that adopt norms that are consistent with optimal single-player strategies with respect to expected reward naturally satisfy a correlated, rather than Nash game theoretic equilibrium condition. Here, we present a version of this theory that clarifies the basic ingredients. We formulate it in the more general Markov game setting more commonly used in reinforcement learning theory. We illustrate the theory by mapping norms over the signal and reward space, while also giving a detailed exposition of the underlying mechanics of the approach. Finally, we give a general solution and analysis of replicator dynamics, which Morsky \& Akçay (2019) propose as a means by which these norms could emerge.

</details>


### [510] [Concave is the New Linear: The Impossibility of Anti-Plutocratic DAO Governance](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.18990)
*Austin Bennett,Preston Vander Vos,Duc V. Le,Mira Belenkiy*

Main category: cs.GT

TL;DR: 本文证明，在无许可区块链上，任何仅依赖钱包余额的投票规则都无法抵御女巫攻击。通过建立包含区块链摩擦成本的链上投票模型，发现即使是旨在削弱大户影响力的凹投票规则（如二次投票），攻击者通过分裂代币仍能获得至少线性增长的投票权。在真实DAO（ENS、Compound、Uniswap、Arbitrum、ZKsync）的回测中，二次投票下的女巫放大因子高达1,172倍至4,039倍，更陡峭的权力规则下甚至超过229,000倍，而攻击成本远低于被攻击资产价值。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 去中心化自治组织（DAO）普遍采用代币加权投票，导致治理权集中于少数大户，已引发多次治理攻击。社区尝试使用反寡头投票机制（如二次投票）来削弱大额持有者的影响力，但这些机制在无许可区块链上是否真正有效，缺乏严格的理论分析和实证检验。

Method: 1) 构建了包含钱包拆分成本、投票成本、固定设置成本和最低余额要求的链上投票成本模型；2) 理论证明了任何仅基于钱包余额的投票规则下，女巫攻击者通过拆分代币可获得至少线性增长的投票权；3) 对ENS、Compound、Uniswap、Arbitrum、ZKsync五大DAO的最近十项提案，在线性、二次、对数和幂律（β=0.25）投票规则下进行回测，计算女巫放大因子。

Result: 理论上，任何正、递增、有限的凹投票规则下，最优攻击策略产生的投票权渐近线性于代币持有量。实证中，二次投票的女巫放大因子为1,172倍至4,039倍，幂律规则下超过229,000倍。所有场景的攻击成本均远低于被治理资产的价值。

Conclusion: 在无许可区块链环境下，仅基于钱包余额设计的任何投票规则（包括反寡头机制）都无法从根本上防止女巫攻击。反寡头投票机制的实际效果被严重高估，DAO治理安全需要重新思考底层设计原则。

Abstract: Decentralized Autonomous Organizations (DAOs) run protocol governance by letting token holders vote on proposals. The dominant rule, voting power proportional to wallet balance, concentrates control among a small number of large holders, fueling the token-control governance attacks that have already compromised real protocols. To counter this concentration, the community has turned to anti-plutocratic voting mechanisms such as Quadratic Voting (QV), which assign sublinear voting power per token with the goal of dampening the influence of large holders.
  We prove that no voting rule that derives power solely from wallet balance can succeed on a permissionless blockchain. Through a costed model of on-chain voting that captures realistic blockchain frictions -- including per-wallet splitting and voting costs, fixed setup costs, and minimum-balance requirements -- we show that whenever a wallet of any size yields nonzero voting power, a Sybil attacker who splits tokens across many wallets achieves total voting power that grows at least linearly in their token holdings. For concave rules actually proposed to dampen governance power -- those that are positive, increasing, and finite -- we show that the optimal strategy yields power that is asymptotically linear in token holdings, regardless of the cost scheme.
  Instantiating the model on real DAOs reveals attack costs orders of magnitude below the value at stake. Replaying the ten most recent finalized proposals of five major DAOs (ENS, Compound, Uniswap, Arbitrum, and ZKsync) under linear, quadratic, logarithmic, and power-($β= 0.25$) voting, we measure Sybil amplification factors between $1,172\times$ and $4,039\times$ under Quadratic Voting, and exceeding $229,000\times$ under steeper power rules.

</details>


### [511] [Nash Welfare in Additively Separable Hedonic Games](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.19030)
*Marta Pagano,Alexander Schlenga*

Main category: cs.GT

TL;DR: 本文首次在可加分离享乐博弈(ASHGs)中研究纳什福利，证明了高纳什福利划分的良好性质（如对称博弈中的契约纳什稳定性），并给出了近似算法（针对AEGs的n-1近似、针对朋友欣赏博弈的2n近似）以及不可近似性界限（NP难，因子1.0000759），同时刻画了在限制联盟规模或数量下的计算复杂度边界。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: ASHGs中社会 welfare 最大化传统上只关注功利主义福利，而纳什福利——一种兼顾公平与效率、具有尺度不变性的重要经济学指标——完全被忽视。本文旨在填补这一空白，系统研究ASHGs中的纳什福利最大化问题。

Method: 通过理论分析证明高纳什福利划分满足契约纳什稳定性等良好性质；设计基于打包(Packing)的近似算法对AEGs和AFGs子类给出近似比；通过归约证明一般ASHGs中纳什福利近似的NP难性及严格不可近似性下界；在限制联盟规模或数量时，通过多项式时间算法与NP难归约刻画计算复杂度边界。

Result: 证明了高纳什福利划分在对称博弈中保证契约纳什稳定性；给出了AEGs的n-1近似算法和AFGs的2n近似算法；证明了在一般ASHGs中NP难近似到1.0000759因子以内；当联盟规模或数量限制为2时多项式时间可解，限制为3或以上时NP难或不可近似。

Conclusion: 纳什福利在ASHGs中是一个富有前景的优化目标，兼具公平性与效率，但计算上具有挑战性；论文给出的近似算法和复杂度边界为后续研究奠定了基础。

Abstract: Additively separable hedonic games (ASHGs) are a prominent model of coalition formation where agents' preferences are derived from their individual valuations of peers. While social welfare maximization in ASHGs has traditionally focused mostly on utilitarian welfare, Nash welfare -- a well-established metric in economics which balances fairness with efficiency and offers scale invariance -- has been entirely overlooked. In this paper, we initiate the study of Nash welfare in ASHGs. We point out desirable properties fulfilled by partitions with high Nash welfare. This includes guaranteed contractual Nash stability in symmetric games, even for any approximation of Nash welfare. This is particularly appealing since, as for other welfare notions, Nash welfare turns out to be NP-hard to maximize, even for the ASHG subclass of symmetric aversion to enemies games (AEGs). A main focus of our study is on approximation algorithms for the Nash welfare objective. We present packing-based algorithms with approximation ratios for well-established subclasses of ASHGs: $n-1$ for AEGs and $2n$ for appreciation of friends games. This is complemented by a strict inapproximability result showing it is NP-hard to approximate Nash welfare within a factor of $1.0000759$ in general ASHGs. Further, we investigate the restricted settings with an upper bound on the coalition size or number of coalitions, and draw the boundary between the cases admitting efficient algorithms and those yielding NP-hardness: bounding the allowed size or number of coalitions by $2$ admits polynomial-time solvability, whereas bounds of $3$ or more yield NP-hardness or unbounded inapproximability.

</details>


### [512] [Distributionally Robust Games via Coherent Risk Measures](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.19302)
*Bharat Gangwani,Arunesh Sinha*

Main category: cs.GT

TL;DR: 本文研究数据驱动博弈中的策略互动，提出分布鲁棒博弈框架，将风险敏感度作为玩家偏好的原始特征，并证明了均衡存在性、计算复杂性（PPAD-完全），给出了多种相干效用度量博弈的互补性规划公式，实验验证了鲁棒性。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 在数据驱动的博弈中，玩家面临从有限样本推断收益分布的不确定性。现有方法缺乏对玩家风险态度（如风险厌恶）的系统建模，需要统一风险理论视角与均衡分析。

Method: 基于分布鲁棒优化框架，结合相干效用度量（如均值-半偏差、条件风险价值CVaR），将风险敏感度嵌入玩家偏好；利用多线性互补规划进行均衡计算，并分析PPAD复杂性。

Result: 证明了数据驱动设定下多种模糊集对应的均衡存在性；揭示了博弈的连续本质；刻画了风险厌恶导致的期望效用损失上界；证明了一般情形下均衡计算的PPAD-完全性；给出了相干效用度量博弈的PPAD成员资格及互补性公式。

Conclusion: 该框架统一了风险理论建模与均衡分析，为数据驱动环境下的风险感知策略决策提供了理论基础。

Abstract: We study strategic interaction in data-driven games where players face uncertainty about payoff distributions inferred from finite samples. To model calibrated attitudes toward such uncertainty, we formulate distributionally robust games with a special focus on coherent utility (risk) measures, including Mean-semideviation and Conditional Value-at-Risk. This framework treats risk sensitivity as a primitive feature of player preferences while retaining a formal connection to distributional robustness. We make a number of contributions that are enumerated next. (1) We use prior results for the existence of distributionally robust equilibria to show the existence of equilibria in data-driven settings for various ambiguity sets, and (2) show that these games are inherently continuous, rather than finite matrix games, which fundamentally alters equilibrium structure and precludes direct extensions of standard correlated equilibrium notions. (3) We bound the loss in expected utility that a player can expect from being risk-averse. (4) We further characterize the computational complexity of equilibrium computation, proving PPAD-completeness in general and PPAD membership for several coherent utility measure games. (5) We present multilinear complementarity program formulations for several coherent utility measure games. (6) Numerical experiments reveal the robustness and out of sample performance of the game solutions. Our results unify risk-theoretic modeling and equilibrium analysis, providing a principled foundation for risk-aware strategic decision-making in data-driven environments.

</details>


### [513] [Competitive Search with a Faulty Satnav (GPS): When Probability Matching is Rational](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.19440)
*Steve Alpern,Mark Broom*

Main category: cs.GT

TL;DR: 本文研究了一个竞争搜索博弈模型：一份可分割宝藏位于网络节点H，n名搜索者从起点出发竞相最先到达H，并与同时到达者平分宝藏。在星形图上，每位搜索者以概率q遵循共同卫星导航仪（以概率p指向H）的指示，并证明了存在唯一的对称均衡q。该均衡q随p和k递增、随n递减，且当n趋于无穷时q趋近于p，为行为科学中的概率匹配现象提供了理性解释。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 传统搜索博弈关注藏匿者与搜索者之间的对抗，但现实场景（如石油勘探、寻找失事飞行员）中，搜索者之间存在竞争关系。本文旨在建立一个多人竞争搜索的博弈模型，研究搜索者之间的策略互动。

Method: 采用博弈论分析方法，考虑星形图上的竞争搜索博弈。搜索者从中心出发，目标H位于k个叶节点之一。每位搜索者拥有共同GPS（以概率p正确指向H），并可自行选择遵循GPS的概率q。通过对称均衡分析，推导出均衡q的表达式及性质。

Result: 存在唯一的对称均衡q；q随p和k递增，随n递减；当n→∞时，q→p，即概率匹配（probability matching）在极限情况下是理性选择。

Conclusion: 本文为竞争搜索博弈中的理性策略提供了理论依据，并揭示了在多人竞争且信息有限的条件下，概率匹配可以是一种纳什均衡行为，拓展了对该行为合理性的理解。

Abstract: A divisible treasure is located at a node $H$ of a network. From a given start node a group of $n$ Searchers each seek to reach $H$ first, dividing the treasure equally with the other first arrivers. This type of search game is called competitive search, where the conflict is not between hider and searcher but between searchers. Examples are search for oil deposits or for a pilot downed over enemy territory. In our model, the Searchers have a common Satnav (GPS) which points to $H$ at each branch node with a known probability $p<1$ and each Searcher chooses the probability $q$ with which they follow the pointer. We consider a family of star graphs where the Searchers start at the center and $H$ lies at one of the $k$ leaf nodes. We show that for all parameter values $n,k,p,$ there is a unique trust probability $q$ which forms a symmetric equilibrium. The equilibrium $q$ is increasing in $p,$ decreasing in $n$ and increasing in $k$. Furthemore for fixed $k$ and $p$ we have $q$ equal to $p$ in the limit of $n$ tending to infinity. This last fact is a new example where what is known in behavioural science as probability matching is in fact rational.

</details>


### [514] [The Privacy Subsidy in Glosten-Milgrom: Bid-Ask Spread and Welfare under Flip-Noise Direction Observation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.19742)
*Yuki Nakamura*

Main category: cs.GT

TL;DR: 本文推导了Glosten-Milgrom 1985序列交易模型在交易方向受二进制翻转信道（概率η）扰动下的闭式买卖价差和福利分解，发现了"隐私补贴"现象，并将该概念从连续高斯市场扩展到离散两状态市场微观结构。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 将信息论隐私机制（二进制翻转信道）引入经典的市场微观结构模型（Glosten-Milgrom 1985），研究隐私扰动对交易价差和福利分配的影响。

Method: 在贝叶斯做市商定价规则下，将交易方向信号通过概率η的二进制翻转信道扰动，推导闭式均衡价差和福利分解。

Result: 均衡价差为μ(1-2η)Δ，每笔交易存在μηΔ的"隐私补贴"从流动性池转移给交易者。

Conclusion: 隐私补贴概念在离散两状态微观结构中同样成立，对基于MPC和差分隐私的匹配引擎设计有指导意义。

Abstract: We derive a closed-form bid-ask spread and welfare decomposition for the Glosten-Milgrom 1985 sequential-trading model when the market maker observes the trade direction perturbed by a binary flip channel of probability $η$ -- a natural information-theoretic model of privacy mechanisms acting on the direction signal. Under a committed Bayesian market-maker pricing rule, the equilibrium spread is $μ(1-2η)Δ$, where $μ$ is the informed-trader fraction and $Δ= v_H - v_L$ the value range. The welfare decomposition identifies a per-trade transfer $μηΔ$ from the protocol's liquidity pool to traders -- the "privacy subsidy", mirroring the Gaussian-Kyle analog established in prior work. The result extends the privacy-subsidy concept from continuous Gaussian to discrete two-state microstructure, demonstrating robustness across both classical models. Primary application: MPC-based matching engines with $\varepsilon$-differentially-private direction disclosure, where the engine prices on a noisy direction signal.

</details>


### [515] [Perpetual Fully Online Horizon-Free Approximate Fairness](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.19844)
*Ido Kahana,Erel Segal-Halevi,Noam Hazon*

Main category: cs.GT

TL;DR: 本文针对长期未知时长的在线决策过程，提出基于"赤字"的通用在线公平框架，并分析了一种无需未来信息的简单全在线规则，证明其可提供随时间增长的$\sqrt{t}$阶松弛保证，适用于在线资源分配和公共决策等场景。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 许多在线决策过程运行时长未知且需即时做出不可撤销的选择，同时公平要求往往具有规模依赖性（即公平尺度随时间变化）。现有方法通常需要知道总轮次或未来信息，难以应对这种动态公平需求。

Method: 提出基于"赤字"（deficit）的通用框架，衡量每个公平要求相对于时变基准的当前短缺；分析一个简单全在线规则，每轮选择最能改善下一轮赤字分布的行动。

Result: 证明该规则具有随时（anytime）保证：每轮后所有追踪要求的松弛度以$\sqrt{t}$（含对数因子）增长，且该增长在一般情况下不可避免。在不可分物品在线分配和在线公共决策中进行了实例化。

Conclusion: 该在线公平规则无需知道时间总长、最大物品价值等未来信息，且保证在每个时间步持续成立，优于以往需要预先知道时间范围的在线公平分配方法。

Abstract: Many decision processes run for a long and unknown duration: in each round new requests arrive, an irrevocable choice must be made immediately, and the system is judged by ongoing fairness requirements. Examples include food banks allocating donated items as they arrive, computing systems repeatedly scheduling scarce resources across users, and institutions making repeated public decisions (e.g., which proposals or cases to prioritize) while remaining fair over time.
  A key challenge in such settings is that fairness requirements are often naturally \emph{scale-dependent}. For example, in fair item allocation, it is common to require that the unfairness is bounded by the highest values of items seen so far. Thus, the scale of fairness changes over time.
  We propose a general approach to online fairness based on \emph{deficits}, which measure each requirement's current shortfall relative to a time-varying benchmark.
  Within this framework, we analyze a simple fully online rule that, in each round, chooses the action that best improves the next-round deficit profile. We prove anytime (prefix-wise) guarantees: after every round, all tracked requirements remain satisfied up to a slack that grows only on the order of $\sqrt{t}$ (up to logarithmic factors), and we show this growth is unavoidable in general. We instantiate the framework for online allocation of indivisible goods (yielding natural relaxations of proportionality and envy-freeness) and for online public decision-making. In contrast to previous works on online fair allocation, our rule does not need to know the horizon (the total number of rounds), nor any other information on the future (e.g. the maximum item value). Moreover, our guarantees hold perpetually, at each individual time step.

</details>


### [516] [Real-Time Parallel Counterfactual Regret Minimization](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.19928)
*Boning Li,Longbo Huang*

Main category: cs.GT

TL;DR: 本文提出Parallel CFR，首个针对实时深度受限CFR求解的并行化框架，通过七阶段流水线、信息集与树节点双维度并行、以及CPU-GPU异构推理，在单桌面设备上将单线程CFR加速3.3-3.4倍，实现了在实时决策预算内完成数百次CFR迭代。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 在实时博弈系统中，求解器必须在严格时间预算（仅数秒）内计算近均衡策略，而CFR迭代次数直接决定游戏强度。现有单线程CFR无法在有限时间内完成足够迭代，亟需并行化加速方案。

Method: 1) 将每次CFR迭代分解为七阶段流水线；2) 识别两个正交并行维度：按信息集并行和按树节点并行；3) 将叶节点评估卸载至GPU进行批处理神经网络推理，构建异构CPU-GPU流水线。

Result: 在HU NLH的翻牌后场景中，相比单线程基线获得3.3-3.4倍加速，每次迭代耗时约47-54ms，深度受限博弈树规模超10亿历史状态，单台桌面设备（NVIDIA DGX Spark）即可运行。

Conclusion: Parallel CFR使得在单台桌面级设备上即可实现实时决策所需的数百次CFR迭代，无需数据中心级基础设施，为实时博弈系统的实用化部署提供了可行方案。

Abstract: Counterfactual Regret Minimization (CFR) is the dominant algorithmic family for solving large imperfect-information games, underpinning breakthroughs such as Libratus and Pluribus in No-Limit Texas Hold'em poker. In real-time game-playing systems, the solver must compute a near-equilibrium strategy within a strict time budget of only a few seconds per decision, and the number of CFR iterations completed in this window directly determines play strength. We present \textbf{Parallel CFR}, the first parallelization framework for real-time depth-limited CFR solving that seamlessly integrates pruning, abstraction, and advanced CFR variants. We decompose each CFR iteration into a pipeline of seven stages and identify two orthogonal dimensions of parallelism: \emph{by information set} and \emph{by tree node}. Leaf node evaluation is offloaded to GPUs via batched neural network inference, creating a heterogeneous CPU--GPU pipeline. Experiments on Heads-Up No-Limit Texas Hold'em demonstrate that Parallel CFR achieves $3.3$--$3.4\times$ speedup over the single-threaded baseline on postflop streets, with per-iteration time of ${\sim}47$--$54$~ms on a depth-limited game tree with over $1$ billion histories. All experiments run on a single desktop-class device (NVIDIA DGX Spark), enabling hundreds of CFR iterations within a typical real-time decision budget without requiring datacenter-scale infrastructure.

</details>


### [517] [Equilibria in Multiplayer Graph Games: An Algorithmic Study](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.19954)
*Léonard Brice*

Main category: cs.GT

TL;DR: 本文研究多人博弈中的五种均衡概念，并针对每个概念，给出在约束存在性问题（即判断是否存在使每个玩家收益在指定区间内的均衡）上的复杂度结果。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有研究多聚焦于两人零和博弈来验证程序或协议的鲁棒性，但实际场景涉及多个系统或多种目标，需要更丰富的多人博弈框架，且需识别均衡策略。

Method: 研究五种均衡概念（包含纳什均衡等），针对每个概念提出约束存在性问题，即判断是否存在均衡使得每个玩家的收益落在给定区间内，并给出相应的复杂度结果。

Result: 针对五种均衡概念，分别给出了约束存在性问题的复杂度结果（如是否为P、NP-hard、PSPACE-complete等）。

Conclusion: 为不同均衡概念下的策略约束问题提供了复杂度分类，有助于理解在实际多人博弈中进行鲁棒策略验证的计算难易程度。

Abstract: To verify the robustness of a program or protocol, it is common in the computer science community to rely on the theoretical framework of game theory. In particular, if one seeks to enforce a desired property, or specification, despite an unpredictable environment, a useful abstraction is to model the situation as a two-player zero-sum game. The goal is then to find a strategy for the system that guarantees the specification against any strategy of the environment. However, to model more complex situations, such as multiple systems with different objectives or an environment composed of various agents, the richer framework of multiplayer games must be considered. In this setting, a natural question is to identify equilibria, i.e., strategy profiles that are robust in the sense that no player has an incentive to deviate. The most well-known equilibrium concept is the Nash equilibrium, but several alternatives exist. We study five such notions and, for each of them, we provide complexity results for the constrained existence problem, which consists of deciding whether a given game contains an equilibrium that ensures each player a payoff within a specified interval.

</details>


<div id='cs.NE'></div>

# cs.NE [[Back]](#toc)

### [518] [GOAL: Graph-based Objective-Aligned Diffusion Solvers for Dynamic Multi-Objective Optimization](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.19119)
*Xingyu Li*

Main category: cs.NE

TL;DR: GOAL是一个基于条件扩散的条件优化求解器，利用异构图编码处理多种约束，在三个调度基准上实现100%可行解且MAPE低于0.20%，推理速度比NSGA-II和MOEA/D快25倍。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有神经组合优化方法只能模仿最优决策，局限于单目标最小化和静态约束，无法灵活应对多目标、多约束的调度问题。

Method: 提出GOAL：基于扩散模型的条件求解器，采用异构图编码，不同边类型对应不同约束类别，通过图神经网络选择性地传播信息，支持根据人类指定目标进行可控决策生成。

Result: 在FSP、JSP、FJSP三个调度基准上，100%可行解，多目标MAPE低于0.20%，推理速度比NSGA-II和MOEA/D快25倍，且无需修改架构即可泛化到不同约束类型。

Conclusion: GOAL证明了条件扩散模型配合异构图编码可有效解决多目标、多约束的组合优化问题，在可行性和多目标精度上显著超越传统进化方法。

Abstract: Existing neural combinatorial optimization solvers frame solution search as imitation of optimal decisions, inherently limiting their utility to single-objective minimization and static constraints. We propose GOAL, a conditioned diffusion solver over relational graph representations that enables controllable decision generations by conditioning on human-specified objectives. We introduce a heterogeneous graph encoding in which distinct edge types, corresponding to different classes of constraints, define the message passing structure of the graph neural network, which allows information to propagate selectively according to the ontology of each constraint. GOAL is instantiated and evaluated on three canonical scheduling benchmarks of various constraint complexity: the Flow Shop Problem (FSP), the Job Shop Scheduling Problem (JSP), and the Flexible Job Shop Scheduling Problem (FJSP). Generalization is demonstrated across structurally distinct constraint regimes and problem types without architectural modification. On all three benchmarks, GOAL achieves 100% solution feasibility and near-zero MAPE (below 0.20%) on multiple objectives for problem sizes up to 20 jobs and 60 operations, outperforming NSGA-II and MOEA/D in both solution quality and inference speed by up to 25x.

</details>


### [519] [Multi-population Diversity-guided Genetic Algorithm for Feature Selection in Network Intrusion Detection](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.19864)
*Chunzhen Li*

Main category: cs.NE

TL;DR: 本文提出了一种多群体多样性引导的遗传算法（MPDGGA），用于解决网络入侵检测系统中高维冗余特征导致的种群多样性难以维持和进化算子缺乏引导的问题。该算法通过链式多群体进化结构和基于信息增益比的多样性引导算子，在NSL-KDD、UNSW-NB15及9个UCI数据集上显著优于四种先进的多群体特征选择模型，在10个数据集上取得最高准确率，且最少仅选择2.26%的特征。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有的基于遗传算法的特征选择方法在处理高维冗余网络流量特征时存在两大问题：种群多样性难以维持，以及进化算子缺乏引导。因此需要提出一种能有效维持多样性并提供进化引导的新方法。

Method: 提出MPDGGA算法，包含两个核心组件：(1) 链式多群体进化结构，通过多个种群的链式协作增强搜索能力；(2) 基于信息增益比的多样性引导算子，为进化过程提供有效引导。

Result: 在NSL-KDD、UNSW-NB15及9个UCI数据集共11个数据集上的实验结果表明：MPDGGA在10个数据集中取得了最高准确率，显著优于四种对比的多群体特征选择模型；在特征选择效率方面，最少仅需选择2.26%的特征即可达到优异性能。

Conclusion: MPDGGA有效解决了遗传算法在特征选择中种群多样性维持和进化引导不足的问题，在网络入侵检测的特征选择任务中表现卓越，能够以极少的特征数量获得高分类准确率。

Abstract: Network Intrusion Detection System is a critical means of ensuring cybersecurity. However, existing Genetic Algorithm-based feature selection methods face several limitations when dealing with high-dimensional redundant traffic features. For example, population diversity is difficult to maintain, and evolutionary operators lack guidance. To solve these problems, this study proposes the Multi-Population Diversity-Guided Genetic Algorithm (MPDGGA). First, we build a chained multi-population evolutionary structure. Second, we introduce a diversity-guided operator based on information gain ratio. Experiments on NSL-KDD, UNSW-NB15, and 9 UCI datasets show that the proposed model significantly outperforms four other advanced multi-population feature selection models. Across the 11 datasets, it attains the highest accuracy on 10 datasets and at least 2.26% of the features were selected.

</details>


### [520] [What Do Evolutionary Coding Agents Evolve?](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.20086)
*Nico Pelleriti,Sree Harsha Nelaturu,Zhanke Zhou,Zongze Li,Max Zimmer,Bo Han,Sebastian Pokutta*

Main category: cs.NE

TL;DR: 本文构建了进化编码轨迹数据集EvoTrace，并提出回放方法EvoReplay，揭示了进化搜索中大部分分数提升仅来自少数编辑类型，且约30%的代码行是字节相同的重复引入，表明基准增益可能源于多种不同机制而非全新算法结构。


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: 现有进化编码系统虽在数学发现与算法设计中表现优异，但人们对其"究竟在进化什么"缺乏理解——分数提升可能来自新算法结构、策略调参、重组既有知识或过拟合评估器，仅看最终分数无法区分这些机制。

Method: 构建EvoTrace数据集（涵盖4种进化框架、推理与非推理模型、16个任务），开发EvoReplay回放方法重构局部搜索状态并实施受控干预，使用LLM-as-judge管道对每次代码编辑标注9种类型之一，并通过盲人重标注验证。

Result: 大多数分数提升仅来自少数编辑类型；存在确定性循环模式：约30%的代码行是之前删除行的字节相同重复引入，几乎出现在每次运行中。

Conclusion: 进化编码代理的基准增益可能源于性质不同的机制，仅部分对应于新算法结构；EvoTrace使得超越最终基准分数的诊断性评估成为可能。

Abstract: Recent work pairs LLMs with evolutionary search to iteratively generate, modify, and select code using task-specific feedback. These systems have produced strong results in mathematical discovery and algorithm design, yet a fundamental question remains: what do they actually evolve? Progress is typically summarized by the best score a run reaches under a task-specific evaluator, but that score can reflect several different mechanisms: new algorithmic structure, re-tuning an existing strategy, recombining ideas already in the model's internal knowledge, or overfitting to the evaluator. Distinguishing these mechanisms requires inspecting the search process itself, not only its final outcome. We introduce EvoTrace, a dataset of evolutionary coding traces spanning four evolutionary frameworks, reasoning and non-reasoning models, and 16 tasks across mathematics and algorithm design. To analyze these traces, we develop EvoReplay, a replay-based methodology that reconstructs the local search states behind high-scoring solutions and tests controlled interventions, including adjusting constants, removing program components and substituting models or prompting contexts. We annotate every code edit in EvoTrace with one of nine recurring edit types using an LLM-as-judge pipeline validated against blind human re-annotation. Across EvoTrace, most score gains come from a small subset of these edit types. We further find a deterministic cycling pattern: about 30% of code lines added during search are byte-identical re-introductions of previously-deleted lines, present throughout nearly every run. These results show that benchmark gains in evolutionary coding agents can arise from qualitatively different mechanisms, only some of which correspond to new algorithmic structure. EvoTrace enables more diagnostic evaluation of evolutionary coding agents beyond final benchmark scores.

</details>
